scope: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v2 hash-stability and dependency-closure verification

inspectionTable.recomputedHash: n/a (published bundle, independent of v2's bundle)

hashVerification.verifyReleaseRecomputedChecksum: PASSED (checksums.sha256 whole-file hash independently verified by `verify-release 0.4.0-pilot`; distinct from, but consistent with, the semantic metadata.contentHash)
hashVerification.computedAcrossRepeatedInvocations: identical (3x same-process, and again in a fresh process after a 1.1s real-time delay)

whyPreviousReportSaidPerRelease: The phrase 'fresh, distinct per-release' in the prior report referred to the fact that v2's hash value had never appeared in any release before 0.4.0-pilot (it is a newly-introduced hash, as opposed to v1's hash which already existed across three prior releases) — NOT that the hash changes from release to release. This was ambiguous wording, not a hashing defect. It is corrected below.

dependencyClosure.verdict: v2's PublishedTemplateBundle contains no reference of documentType TEMPLATE_COMBINATION anywhere in its own dependency fields — v1 is not, and structurally cannot be, a dependency of v2.

whyBothV1AndV2AppearIn040Pilot.cause: CatalogPublisher.BuildRelease builds one PublishedTemplateBundle PER combination present in the full catalog source snapshot (src/PlanCatalog.Infrastructure/Publishing/CatalogPublisher.cs, `stamped.Combinations.Select(c => bundleAssembler.Assemble(...))`), and separately republishes every stamped catalog document (including every PlanTemplate/RunLayout/.../Combination version) into the release's per-type folders. Because catalog/combinations/ contains both ten-k-4d-intermediate.v1.json and .v2.json, every release built from this source produces two independent bundles: one rooted at combination v1, one rooted at combination v2.
whyBothV1AndV2AppearIn040Pilot.classification: C — accidentally included in the sense that the publisher packages every catalog file/combination, not because v1 is a dependency of v2 or because of any selection logic bug
whyBothV1AndV2AppearIn040Pilot.briefEvidence: plan-catalog-antigravity-brief-v2.md §15: 'Release manifest: Publish edilen bütün artifact ve bundle'ları listeler' (the release manifest lists ALL published artifacts and bundles) — full-catalog packaging per release is the documented design, not single-bundle selective publishing.

reasonNoCorrectionNeeded: Hash is proven deterministic across source, bundle assembly, and the published release (identical value in all three, and stable across repeated computation and elapsed wall-clock time). The v2 bundle's dependency closure contains no reference to combination v1 or to any TEMPLATE_COMBINATION document at all. Both findings match the intended, documented full-catalog release design. Only prior report wording was corrected.
